lines.en_lit: The Gemara raises an objection. and they shall put on other garments, so that they do not sanctify the people with their garments”
What, doesn’t “other” more important than No, “other” inferior to them,

Rav Huna bar Yehuda, and some say Rav Shmuel bar Yehuda, taught: After the public service concluded, a priest whose mother had made him tunic wear it and perform an individual service it, provided he transfers it to the public. obvious;

Lest you say perhaps and will not transfer it wholeheartedly; it teaches us They said about Rabbi Yishmael ben Pabi that his mother made him a tunic worth one hundred maneh. He donned it and performed an individual service and transferred to the public.

they said about Rabbi Elazar ben Ḥarsum that his mother made him a tunic twenty thousand but his fellow priests did not allow him to wear it because he appeared as naked. And could be seen Didn’t the Master say: The threads were six-fold? Abaye said: It is like wine in glass

the Sages taught: A poor and a wealthy and a wicked come to judgment To the poor say: Why did you not engage in Torah? If he and says: I was poor and preoccupied with my sustenance they say to him: Were you any poorer than Hillel,

They said about Hillel the Elder that each and every day he would work and earn a half-dinar, half of which he would give to the guard of the study hall and half of which for his sustenance and the sustenance of the members of his family. One time he did not find to earn and the guard of the study hall did not allow him to enter. He ascended suspended and sat at the edge of the skylight in order to hear the words of the living God from the mouths of Shemaya and Avtalyon,

said: That day was Shabbat eve and it was the season of Tevet, and snow fell upon him from the sky. When it was dawn, Shemaya said to Avtalyon: Avtalyon, my brother, every day the hall bright and today it is dark; is it perhaps a cloudy day? They focused their eyes and saw the image of a man in the skylight. They ascended and found him snow three cubits high. They extricated him and they washed him and smeared on him, and they sat him opposite the bonfire They said: This is worthy to desecrate Shabbat for him.

a wealthy say to him: Why did you not engage in Torah? If he says: I was wealthy and preoccupied with my possessions, they say to him: Were you any wealthier than Rabbi Elazar, They said about Rabbi Elazar ben Ḥarsum that his father left him one thousand villages on land, and corresponding to them, one thousand ships at sea. And each and every day he takes jug of flour on his shoulder and walks from city to city and from state to state to study Torah

One time his servants found him, and, they pressed him into service [angarya] He said to them: I beseech you; let me be and I will go study Torah. They said: by the life of Rabbi Elazar ben Ḥarsum that will not let you be. And in all his days, he never went and saw rather, sit and engage in Torah all day and all night.

say to him: Why did you not engage in Torah? If he said: I was handsome and preoccupied with my inclination, they say to him: Were you any more handsome than Joseph, They said about Joseph the righteous: Each and every day, the wife of Potiphar seduced him with words. the clothes that she wore to him in the morning, she did not wear to him in the evening. The clothes that she wore to him in the evening, she did not wear to him in the morning

she said to him: Submit to me He said to her: No. She said to him: I will incarcerate you in the prison. He said to her: “God releases prisoners” She said to him: I will bent over “God straightens those who are bent over” I will blind your eyes. “God opens the eyes of the blind” She gave him a thousand talents of silver to submit to her, “to lie with her and be with her” and he refused.
to lie with her in this world, be with her in the World-to-Come. Consequently, Hillel obligates the poor Rabbi Elazar ben Ḥarsum obligates the wealthy, Joseph obligates the wicked.

MISHNA: comes next to his bull, and his bull was standing between the Entrance Hall and the altar its head to the south and its face to the west. And the priest stands to the east and his face to the west. And places his two hands on and confesses.

And this is what he would say Please, God, I have sinned, I have done wrong, I have rebelled before You, I and my family. Please, God, grant atonement, please, for the sins, and for the wrongs, and for the rebellions that I have sinned, and done wrong, and rebelled before You, I and my family, as it is written in the Torah of Moses your servant: “For on this day atonement shall be made And respond after he Blessed be the name of His glorious kingdom forever and all time.
